places.name: Stockholm Metro Art Gallery
places.description: Stockholm's decidedly unique public transit system has doubled as the world's longest art gallery for decades.

places.name: Codex Gigas (The Devil's Bible)
places.description: An illuminated manuscript comprising the life's work of a lone monk, inexplicably decorated with a portrait of Satan himself.

places.name: R1 Nuclear Reactor
places.description: Sweden's first nuclear reactor, R1, can be visited 25 meters underground.

places.name: Aifur
places.description: Dine on food tied to Viking history and legend at this subterranean Stockholm spot.

places.name: Victoriahuset (Victoria House)
places.description: A rare surviving Victorian greenhouse designed specifically to house giant water lilies.

places.name: Vasa Museum
places.description: It houses the remains of a 17th-century version of the Titanic.

places.name: Kungsträdgården Metro Station
places.description: Stockholm's deepest subway station is also a stunning ecological wonder.

places.name: Stadshuskällaren
places.description: Eat a Nobel banquet from any year in the basement of Stockholm's City Hall.

places.name: Omnipollo's Church
places.description: Located in an old church, this brewery produces a different kind of holy water.
